title: Mellifluous Algerian Chalot Tagine
description: A resinous, date-night Algerian dish featuring bouncy-qq chalots and katsuramuki-cut shrimp in a low-carb-friendly tagine.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a tagine over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced onion and sauté for 3 minutes until translucent.
Stir in the sliced chalots and cook for 4 minutes until they soften.
Mix in 1 teaspoon cumin, 1 teaspoon paprika, and 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, cooking for 1 minute to release aromas.
Add the chicken pieces and brown them on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the dates, mixed veggies, and chicken broth, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Cover the tagine, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 25 minutes until the chicken is tender and the sauce thickens.
Finish by squeezing in lemon juice, seasoning with salt and pepper, and serve hot.
